persona_prompt:
You are filtering Boston, Cambridge, and Providence RI events for Kirk Brown.

Kirk's background:
- Hardware engineer who has also done software engineering and software sales
- Sales Engineer at Mapbox (SoftBank-backed geo/spatial tech) and Bit Flow (machine vision / industrial hardware)
- Experienced selling and demoing both hardware products and software platforms to technical buyers
- Finishing his master's degree
- Looking for his next role — open to hardware, software, or technical sales

What Kirk wants:
- Hardware & embedded: semiconductor events, robotics, embedded systems, IoT, machine vision, industrial tech, PCB/FPGA, sensors
- Defense & deep tech: defense tech startups, dual-use hardware, MIT Lincoln Lab adjacent events, Draper Lab, MITRE
- VC & startup events: demo days, pitch nights, investor panels, founder dinners, accelerator showcases (hardware AND software)
- Tech community: hackathons, product launches, startup meetups, tech networking with practitioners
- Sales Engineer / presales: SE community events, RevOps meetups, Pavilion events, Salesforce/HubSpot ecosystem
- Geographic range: Boston, Cambridge, Somerville, Providence RI — has a car, will travel for quality
- University: MIT, Harvard, Brown, Northeastern, WPI — especially hardware/robotics/EE research events open to industry
- Career: panels with engineering leadership, CTO talks, startup hiring, technical founder events
- Domain expertise: geo/spatial tech, machine vision, industrial automation, fintech, data infrastructure

SCORE 9-10 (must attend):
- Hardware startup demo days or pitch nights (Greentown Labs, FORGE RI, MIT hardware)
- Semiconductor or chip design events (ARM, Analog Devices, MIT MTL, IEEE Boston)
- Robotics showcases with industry participation (Boston Dynamics adjacent, MassRobotics)
- Defense tech or dual-use hardware networking (Draper, MITRE, DIU events open to industry)
- Sales Engineer or presales community events (Pavilion SE chapter, SE Collective)
- VC-hosted events open to founders/operators — hardware or deep tech focus especially
- MIT or Brown research-to-industry events with real networking

SCORE 5-7 (worth knowing):
- General tech networking with solid company mix (not just recruiters)
- University EE/CS/robotics showcases with industry crossover
- Revenue/GTM panels (sales ops, CRO, B2B technical sales)
- SaaS or hardware product launches in relevant sectors
- Maker/hardware community events (Artisan's Asylum, HackerSpace Boston)

LOWER PRIORITY (score ≤ 4):
- Pure academic lectures with no networking or industry angle
- Social events with no career/tech relevance
- Events clearly targeted at undergrads only with no industry presence
- Sports, arts-only, food/drink without tech angle
- Generic networking mixers with no engineering or sales focus

events.name: Open Project Night - Every Tuesday, In Person!
events.description:
Welcome to Cambridge Hackspace!

Bring yourself, your ideas, and your projects—any projects!—to the Hackspace and hang out with kindred spirits who like to tinker, learn, and share ideas about making stuff!

No experience necessary, and no existing project required! We're a place and a community when you can explore and develop your curiosity and interest in making things. Come try things out, learn new skills, and meet folks who want to do the same.

Our door can be hard to find! There are [dir
events.reason: Cambridge Hackspace Open Project Night—hands-on making and tinkering community. Good for hardware/embedded interests and meeting people who build things. Regular, accessible event.

events.name: OpenClaw Meetup
events.description:
## Build Your OpenClaw Strategy**.**

### What It Is. Why It Matters. Why It’s Coming Fast.

We are entering a new era of computing.
Not apps.
Not dashboards.
Not copilots.

**Agents.**
Autonomous systems that reason, plan, call tools, interact with environments, and work alongside humans. But for agents to actually operate in the real world, they need something we have not fully defined yet:

**An operating system for agents.**
This is where **OpenClaw** comes in.
OpenClaw represents a new way
events.reason: OpenClaw is an autonomous agents/AI operating systems platform—directly relevant to Kirk's software engineering, deep tech, and startup ecosystem interests. Agent infrastructure is a key emerging area; good networking opportunity with technical founders and practitioners.

events.name: Beyond Vibe Coding: Building Multi-Agent Systems with MCP & A2A
events.description:
Everyone's talking about AI agents. Frameworks are multiplying. Demos are everywhere. But how do these systems actually work under the hood? Val Andrei Fajardo dismantles the magic by building a multi-agent system from first principles. No framework dependency, no black boxes. Just the core mechanics that every agent framework is built on, exposed and explained.

**What you'll learn:**

* The foundational agent building blocks: how an LLM + tools + a reasoning loop becomes the basic unit of ever
events.reason: Multi-agent systems and AI architecture talk. Kirk's software engineering background and technical depth make this relevant; agent frameworks are emerging infrastructure tech.

events.name: Brookline Rust Lunch, Apr 11
events.description:
Join us for a casual lunchtime get-together to chat about Rust, the universe, and everything.

**WHEN:** Saturday, April 11. We'll be there from 11:00 to 1:00, but feel free to stay and hang out for as long or as little as you like.

**WHERE:** Temptations Cafe near Coolidge Corner in Brookline.

**MORE EVENTS:** We host regular lunches (see the map above for the spot nearest to you), weekend hackathons, and large evening meetups. See [our events page](https://www.meetup.com/bostonrust/events/?t
events.reason: Rust community lunch in Brookline. Local tech practitioner meetup with engineers; Rust is used in embedded systems, robotics, and systems-level hardware work. Strong networking potential.

restaurants.name: Zuma
restaurants.editorial: High-energy Japanese izakaya in the Seaport. Known for a lively bar scene, beautiful room, and a crowd that dresses up. Popular for late dinner and drinks.

restaurants.name: Desnuda
restaurants.editorial: Intimate raw bar and natural wine spot. Industry crowd, low-key cool. East Boston location and Seaport outpost.
